title: Dinner Favors, To S. B. F.
content:
Give me but a bit to eat,
         And an hour or two,
     Just a salad and a sweet,
         And a chat with you.
     Give me table full or bare,
         Crust or rich ragout;
     But whatever be the fare,
         Always give me you.
meaning:
Arthur Macy's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Dinner Favors, To S. B. F."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
